notes: Negative cases that exercise the format-neutral diagnostic-code reduction at the layer-open dispatch boundary. Each case asserts the format-neutral code per parser-diagnostics-mapping.contract.json#code_reduction_table, NOT the USDC-specific wire-layer code. Unit-level adapters preserve the wire-layer code; this integration boundary surfaces the reduced code.

cases.notes: Fixture's sole non-layer SPECS entry has form=0 (Unknown). Form 0 is inert per the crate-section-layouts contract's SPECS spec_form_table; the parser processes it without emitting a diagnostic and does not materialize it as a document-model spec. The resulting layer has no root prims. THIS FIELD IS DOCUMENTATION ONLY (case-level, not inside `expected`); score.py does not compare it.

Adversarial counterpart to invalid-spec-form-twelve-reduces-to-malformed-spec-form. Same malformation (form=12 patched into the SPECS forms array) but the prim is named /Foo instead of /World, and the layer also contains a sibling /Helper prim. The parser must detect form=12 from the SPECS forms array itself, not from the presence of any specific token name in TOKENS or any specific path in PATHS. A parser that fingerprints by searching for 'World' / 'Dup' / 'WorldHelper' in tokens or paths (as the regen on 2026-05-28 did) silently produces ok=true on this fixture and fails this case.

diagnostics.message: spec form value outside the supported set

cases.notes: Adversarial counterpart to duplicate-spec-path-diagnostic. Same malformation (path_indices patched so two SPECS entries resolve to the same path) but the duplicated path is /Bar instead of /Dup. The parser must detect duplicates by tracking emitted spec paths through usd-document-model and propagating add_spec's DuplicateSpecPath diagnostic to the dump, not by searching for any specific token or path name. A parser that fingerprints by checking for 'Dup' in tokens (as the regen on 2026-05-28 did) silently produces ok=true on this fixture and fails this case.
